intro: Python manages structural automation logic and application backends via explicit object validation. Bash functions directly within the operating system shell to orchestrate commands, file system streams, and lightweight process lifecycles. System administrators move code from Python to Bash to eliminate package dependencies and create fast, direct deployment automation.

diffs.from: General-purpose high-level programming and scripting abstractions.
diffs.to: Command-line execution, stream redirection, and process control.
String-based variables; untyped data evaluated contextually.

diffs.from: Explicit return values or typed programmatic exceptions.
diffs.to: Process exit status codes (0 for success, non-zero for errors).

diffs.from: Indentation levels define structural program flow blocks.
diffs.to: Command termination delimiters, keywords (fi, done), and semi-colons.

diffs.from: Requires subprocess module imports to execute shell commands.
diffs.to: Executes operating system binary tools directly as native commands.

examples.title: Check File Existence
examples.description: Checking for the presence of a file before initiating operations.
examples.toCode:
if [ -f "app.log" ]; then
    echo "Found"
else
    echo "Missing"
fi

examples.title: Loop Through Arguments
examples.description: Iterating over command-line arguments passed into the execution runtime.
examples.toCode:
for arg in "\$@"; do
    echo "Arg: $arg"
done

tips.title: Quote Variable References
tips.body: Wrap all variable expansions in double quotes (e.g., "\$var") to preserve spaces and avoid globbing issues.

tips.title: Monitor Exit Status Codes
tips.body: Evaluate the \$? parameter immediately after executing a command to catch and handle system failures.

tips.title: Set Safe Execution Modes
tips.body: Include "set -euo pipefail" at the start of scripts to terminate execution early when commands fail.

tips.title: Treat Types as Strings
tips.body: Remember that Bash stores all variable values as strings; use arithmetic expansion ((x++)) for calculations.
